@@ -37,6 +37,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1dez.7","title":"Formula versioning: Version field and git tag integration","description":"Add versioning support to formulas for tracking updates.

## Formula Version Field
```json
{
  "formula": "mol-code-review",
  "version": "1.2.0",
  "min_bd_version": "0.25.0",
  ...
}
```

## Version Semantics
- Use semver: MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH
- MAJOR: Breaking changes to step structure
- MINOR: New optional steps or variables
- PATCH: Documentation, bug fixes

## Git Tag Integration
- `bd mol install repo@v1.2.0` fetches that tag
- `bd mol install repo` fetches latest tag (or main if no tags)
- `bd mol publish --tag v1.2.0` creates tag

## Proto Version Tracking
Local proto should store:
```
source_repo: github.com/anthropics/mol-code-review
source_version: v1.2.0
installed_at: 2025-12-25T12:00:00Z
```

This enables `bd mol update` to check for newer versions.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-25T12:06:42.674849-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T19:43:22.501926-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-25T19:43:22.501926-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1dez.7","depends_on_id":"bd-1dez","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2025-12-25T12:06:42.67694-08:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1dez.8","title":"Installation tracking: .beads/installed.json","description":"Track installed formulas for update checking and provenance.

## File Format
```json
{
  "mol-code-review": {
    "source": "github.com/anthropics/mol-code-review",
    "version": "v1.2.0",
    "installed_at": "2025-12-25T12:00:00Z",
    "formula_hash": "abc123..."
  },
  "mol-polecat-work": {
    "source": "local",
    "version": "1",
    "installed_at": "2025-12-24T10:00:00Z"
  }
}
```

## Commands That Update This
- `bd mol install` - Adds entry
- `bd mol update` - Updates version/timestamp
- `bd cook` - Adds entry with source:local
- `bd mol uninstall` - Removes entry (new command)

## Usage
```bash
bd mol list --installed    # Shows installed with source/version
bd mol outdated            # Shows formulas with available updates
```

## Sync Behavior
- installed.json is gitignored (local state)
- Only formulas/ directory is synced
- Each clone tracks its own installations

 {"id":"bd-1eak","title":"Add bd refile command to move issues between rigs","description":"Add a `bd refile <source-id> <target-rig>` command to move issues between rigs.

Beads already has the routing infrastructure:
- ResolveBeadsDirForID - routes reads by prefix
- ResolveBeadsDirForRig - routes writes by rig name
- GetRoutedStorageForID - opens cross-rig storage connections

Implementation:
1. Get source issue via routing
2. Create new issue in target rig (existing --prefix mechanism)
3. Close source issue with 'Refiled to <new-id>'
4. Optionally: migrate deps, link the two issues

This is pure beads - no gastown involvement needed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-31T12:29:18.161088-08:00","created_by":"beads/crew/grip","updated_at":"2025-12-31T12:34:23.548938-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T12:34:23.548938-08:00","close_reason":"Implemented bd refile command"}

 {"id":"bd-1slh","title":"Investigate charmbracelet-based TUI for beads","description":"Now that we've merged the create-form command (PR #603) which uses charmbracelet/huh, investigate whether beads should have a more comprehensive TUI.

Considerations:
- Should this be in core or a separate binary (bd-tui)?
- What functionality would benefit from a TUI? (list view, issue details, search, bulk operations)
- Plugin/extension architecture vs build tags vs separate binary
- Dependency cost vs user experience tradeoff
- Target audience: humans who want interactive workflows vs CLI/scripting users

Related: PR #603 added charmbracelet/huh dependency for create-form command.","notes":"Foundation is in place (lipgloss, huh), but not a priority right now","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-17T14:20:51.503563-08:00","updated_at":"2026-01-02T00:17:14.232446-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-49kw","title":"Workaround for FastMCP outputSchema bug in Claude Code","description":"The beads MCP server (v0.23.1) successfully connects to Claude Code, but all tools fail to load with a schema validation error due to a bug in FastMCP 2.13.1.

Error: "Invalid literal value, expected \"object\"" in outputSchema.

Root Cause: FastMCP generates outputSchema with $ref at root level without "type": "object" for self-referential models (Issue).

Workaround: Use slash commands (/beads:ready) or wait for FastMCP fix.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-20T18:55:39.041831-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-23T23:49:44.371623-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-23T23:49:44.371623-08:00"}
 {"id":"bd-49oe","title":"Cache GetGitDir() for additional optimization","description":"Code review finding from bd-7di fix.

GetGitDir() at line 17 is not cached but is called by:
- GetGitHooksDir()
- GetGitRefsDir()
- GetGitHeadPath()

If any of those are called multiple times, we still spawn extra git processes. Consider adding sync.Once caching similar to the other functions.

File: internal/git/gitdir.go","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/granite","created_at":"2025-12-25T22:08:52.736529-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T22:14:16.659122-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-30T22:14:16.659122-08:00","close_reason":"Already implemented. GetGitDir() uses getGitContext() which is cached via sync.Once. The gitContext struct (line 13-19) holds gitDir and is populated once by initGitContext(). All dependent functions (GetGitHooksDir, GetGitRefsDir, GetGitHeadPath) benefit from this caching. No changes needed."}

-{"id":"bd-4bt1","title":"Add gate field to Step schema in formula types","description":"Add Gate field to Step struct in internal/formula/types.go.

## Implementation

```go
type Step struct {
    // ... existing fields ...
    
    // Gate defines an async wait condition for this step.
    Gate *GateDef `json:"gate,omitempty"`
}

type GateDef struct {
    Type     string `json:"type"`              // timer, gh:run, gh:pr, bead, human
    Workflow string `json:"workflow,omitempty"` // GitHub workflow name
    Timeout  string `json:"timeout,omitempty"`  // Max wait before escalation
    AwaitID  string `json:"await_id,omitempty"` // Explicit condition ID
}
```

 {"id":"bd-4ec8","title":"Widespread double JSON encoding bug in daemon mode RPC calls","description":"Multiple CLI commands had the same double JSON encoding bug found in bd-1048. All commands that called ResolveID via RPC used string(resp.Data) instead of properly unmarshaling the JSON response. This caused IDs to retain JSON quotes ("bd-1048" instead of bd-1048), which then got double-encoded when passed to subsequent RPC calls.

Affected commands:
- bd show (3 instances)
- bd dep add/remove/tree (5 instances)
- bd label add/remove/list (3 instances)
- bd reopen (1 instance)

Root cause: resp.Data is json.RawMessage (already JSON-encoded), so string() conversion preserves quotes.

Fix: Replace all string(resp.Data) with json.Unmarshal(resp.Data, &id) for proper deserialization.

All commands now tested and working correctly with daemon mode.","status":"tombstone","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-11-02T22:33:01.632691-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"bug"}
 {"id":"bd-4hn","title":"wish: list & ready show issues as hierarchy tree","description":"`bd ready` and `bd list` just show a flat list, and it's up to the reader to parse which ones are dependent or sub-issues of others. It would be much easier to understand if they were shown in a tree format","status":"hooked","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-08T06:38:24.016316945-07:00","updated_at":"2025-12-30T15:44:43.343883-08:00"}
-{"id":"bd-4k3c","title":"bd cook creates gate beads for gate steps","description":"When cooking a formula, steps with Gate field create corresponding gate beads.

## Behavior
1. During cook, detect steps with Gate != nil
2. Create gate bead with ID: `<mol-id>.gate-<step-id>`
3. Gate bead has:
   - issue_type: gate
   - await_type: from Gate.Type
   - await_id: from Gate.AwaitID (or auto-discovered)
   - timeout: from Gate.Timeout
4. Dependent steps get blocking dep on gate bead

## Example
Step `await-ci` with gate.type="gh:run" creates:
- bd-mol-xxx.gate-await-ci (type=gate, await_type=gh:run)
- bd-mol-xxx.verify-github depends on bd-mol-xxx.gate-await-ci

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Gate beads created during cook
- [ ] Correct await_type/await_id populated
- [ ] Dependent steps blocked by gate
- [ ] Tests for gate creation","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-02T13:27:14.512549-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-01-02T13:27:14.512549-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4k3c","depends_on_id":"bd-4bt1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-02T13:28:08.39967-08:00","created_by":"mayor"},{"issue_id":"bd-4k3c","depends_on_id":"bd-d1n1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-02T13:28:18.575284-08:00","created_by":"mayor"}]}
+{"id":"bd-4k3c","title":"bd cook creates gate beads for gate steps","description":"When cooking a formula, steps with Gate field create corresponding gate beads.

## Behavior
1. During cook, detect steps with Gate != nil
2. Create gate bead with ID: `<mol-id>.gate-<step-id>`
3. Gate bead has:
   - issue_type: gate
   - await_type: from Gate.Type
   - await_id: from Gate.AwaitID (or auto-discovered)
   - timeout: from Gate.Timeout
4. Dependent steps get blocking dep on gate bead

## Example
Step `await-ci` with gate.type="gh:run" creates:
- bd-mol-xxx.gate-await-ci (type=gate, await_type=gh:run)
- bd-mol-xxx.verify-github depends on bd-mol-xxx.gate-await-ci

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Gate beads created during cook
- [ ] Correct await_type/await_id populated
- [ ] Dependent steps blocked by gate
- [ ] Tests for gate creation","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"beads/polecats/quartz","created_at":"2026-01-02T13:27:14.512549-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-01-02T13:59:57.696282-08:00","closed_at":"2026-01-02T13:59:57.696282-08:00","close_reason":"Implementation already existed in cook.go (createGateIssue + collectSteps). Added comprehensive tests in cook_test.go covering: gate issue creation, await_type/await_id population, blocking dependencies, parent-child relationships, and timeout parsing.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-4k3c","depends_on_id":"bd-4bt1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-02T13:28:08.39967-08:00","created_by":"mayor"},{"issue_id":"bd-4k3c","depends_on_id":"bd-d1n1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-02T13:28:18.575284-08:00","created_by":"mayor"}]}
 {"id":"bd-4lm3","title":"Correction: Pinned field already in v0.31.0","description":"Quick correction - the Pinned field is already in the current bd v0.31.0:

```go
// In beads internal/types/types.go
Pinned bool `json:"pinned,omitempty"`
```

So you just need to:
1. Add `Pinned bool `json:"pinned,omitempty"`` to BeadsMessage in types.go
2. Sort pinned messages first in listBeads() after fetching

No migration needed - the field is already there.

-- Mayor","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"message","created_at":"2025-12-20T17:52:27.321458-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-21T17:52:18.617995-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-21T17:52:18.617995-08:00","labels":["from:beads-crew-dave","thread:thread-4dd70157dbc1"]}

 {"id":"bd-4nzq","title":"bd create --rig flag for cross-rig issue filing","description":"## Problem

To create an issue in a different rig, you must cd to that rig's directory. This is clunky when coordinating across rigs.

## Proposed Solution

Add a --rig flag to bd create that leverages existing routes.jsonl:

```bash
# From anywhere in town
bd create --rig beads --title="bug report" --type=bug

# Equivalent to:
cd ~/gt/beads/mayor/rig && bd create --title="..."
```

## How it works

1. Parse --rig flag (rig name, not prefix)
2. Look up rig in routes.jsonl to find beads location
3. Determine prefix from rig config
4. Create issue in that location with auto-generated ID

## Why elegant

- Leverages existing routing infrastructure
- Rig names are human-memorable (not cryptic prefixes)
- Works from anywhere in town
- Prefix auto-determined from rig config
- Reads naturally: "create in beads"

## Alternative

Also consider --prefix for power users who know prefixes:
```bash
bd create --prefix bd "Title here"
```","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-12-27T00:39:02.233664-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2025-12-27T00:44:04.079867-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-27T00:44:04.079867-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-htwx","title":"Stale daemon.lock files cause 5+ second delays","description":"## Problem
When a bd daemon crashes or is killed without cleanup, it leaves a stale daemon.lock file. Subsequent bd commands try to connect to the dead daemon, causing 5+ second timeouts before falling back to direct mode.

Found during gt status optimization (gt-zdtpw) - one stale wyvern daemon was causing 10+ second delays.

## Evidence
```
# Lock files with stale PIDs:
$ find ~/gt -name "daemon.lock" -exec cat {} \;
{"pid": 11045, ...}  # Not running (from Dec 27)
{"pid": 15986, ...}  # Not running (from Dec 27)
{"pid": 98055, ...}  # Just restarted

# But running daemons have different PIDs:
$ ps aux | grep "bd.*daemon"
11847, 11683, 11624  # None match the lock files!
```

## Proposed fixes

### 1. Validate lock on startup (quick fix)
Before trusting daemon.lock, check if the PID is still running:
```python
def is_daemon_alive(lock_path):
    lock = json.load(open(lock_path))
    try:
        os.kill(lock["pid"], 0)  # Signal 0 = check if alive
        return True
    except OSError:
        return False
```

### 2. Heartbeat mechanism (robust fix)
Daemon writes periodic heartbeat to lock file or separate file.
Clients check heartbeat age before attempting IPC.

### 3. Lock cleanup in daemon startup
When starting a new daemon, always check for and clean stale locks.

### 4. Graceful shutdown handling
Register signal handlers (SIGTERM, SIGINT) to clean up lock on exit.

## Workaround
For now, gastown uses `--no-daemon` for all read operations to avoid this issue.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2025-12-31T12:19:37.758373-08:00","created_by":"gastown/crew/max","updated_at":"2025-12-31T13:03:56.641703-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-31T13:03:56.641703-08:00","close_reason":"Fixed - use flock-based check as authoritative source instead of PID file"}
 {"id":"bd-hulf","title":"Molecule execution state management","description":"Implement molecule execution state tracking.

## State Location

```
.beads/molecules/<mol-id>.state.yaml
```

## State Schema

```yaml
id: mol-deacon-patrol
formula: mol-deacon-patrol  # Source formula
bonded_at: ISO timestamp
bonded_by: entity who created it

# Execution state
status: running | paused | completed | failed
current_step: step-id
started_at: ISO timestamp
completed_at: ISO timestamp (if done)

# Loop tracking
reset_count: 0
last_reset_at: null

# Per-step state
steps:
  inbox-check:
    status: completed | in_progress | pending
    started_at: ...
    completed_at: ...
  spawn-work:
    status: pending
  self-inspect:
    status: pending

# Variables (from formula instantiation)
variables:
  rig: gastown
  issue_id: gt-xxx
```

## Operations

- CreateState(molID, formula, variables) - Initialize state
- LoadState(molID) - Read current state  
- SaveState(molID, state) - Write state
- AdvanceStep(molID) - Mark current complete, find next
- ResetState(molID) - Reset all steps to pending

## Files

- internal/mol/state.go
- internal/mol/types.go","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-24T15:53:43.381634-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-24T16:53:11.807645-08:00","closed_at":"2025-12-24T16:53:11.807645-08:00"}

 {"id":"bd-hy9p","title":"Add --body-file flag to bd create for reading descriptions from files","description":"## Problem

Creating issues with long/complex descriptions via CLI requires shell escaping gymnastics:

```bash
# Current workaround - awkward heredoc quoting
bd create --title="..." --description="$(cat <<'EOF'
...markdown...
EOF
)"

# Often fails with quote escaping errors in eval context
# Agents resort to writing temp files then reading them
```

## Proposed Solution

Add `--body-file` and `--description-file` flags to read description from a file, matching `gh` CLI pattern.

```bash
# Natural pattern that aligns with training data
cat > /tmp/desc.md << 'EOF'
...markdown content...
EOF

bd create --title="..." --body-file=/tmp/desc.md
```

## Implementation

### 1. Add new flags to `bd create`

```go
createCmd.Flags().String("body-file", "", "Read description from file (use - for stdin)")
createCmd.Flags().String("description-file", "", "Alias for --body-file")
```

### 2. Flag precedence

- If `--body-file` or `--description-file` is provided, read from file
- If value is `-`, read from stdin
- Otherwise fall back to `--body` or `--description` flag
- If neither provided, description is empty (current behavior)

### 3. Error handling

- File doesn't exist → clear error message
- File not readable → clear error message
- stdin specified but not available → clear error message

## Benefits

✅ **Matches training data**: `gh issue create --body-file file.txt` is a common pattern
✅ **No shell escaping issues**: File content is read directly
✅ **Works with any content**: Markdown, special characters, quotes, etc.
✅ **Agent-friendly**: Agents already write complex content to temp files
✅ **User-friendly**: Easier for humans too when pasting long descriptions

## Related Commands

Consider adding similar support to:
- `bd update --body-file` (for updating descriptions)
- `bd comment --body-file` (if/when we add comments)

## Examples

```bash
# From file
bd create --title="Add new feature" --body-file=feature.md

# From stdin
echo "Quick description" | bd create --title="Bug fix" --body-file=-

# With other flags
bd create \
  --title="Security issue" \
  --type=bug \
  --priority=0 \
  --body-file=security-report.md \
  --label=security
```

## Testing

- Test with normal files
- Test with stdin (`-`)
- Test with non-existent files (error handling)
- Test with binary files (should handle gracefully)
- Test with empty files (valid - empty description)
- Test that `--description-file` and `--body-file` are equivalent aliases","status":"tombstone","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2025-11-22T00:02:08.762684-08:00","updated_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_at":"2025-12-25T01:21:01.952723-08:00","deleted_by":"batch delete","delete_reason":"batch delete","original_type":"feature"}

-{"id":"bd-quw1","title":"Polecat phase handoff: gt done --phase-complete","description":"Add phase handoff protocol for polecats hitting gate steps.

## Current Behavior
Polecat runs all steps, blocks on external waits, times out.

## New Behavior
1. Polecat completes work up to gate step
2. Polecat runs: gt done --phase-complete
3. This signals: 'phase done, molecule awaiting gate'
4. Witness recycles polecat (context freed)
5. When gate closes, Witness dispatches new polecat
6. New polecat continues from step after gate

## Implementation
- Add --phase-complete flag to gt done
- Witness tracks molecules in 'awaiting-gate' state
- When gate closes (via bd gate resolve), molecule unblocks
- Dispatch logic resumes molecule with new polecat

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] gt done --phase-complete implemented
- [ ] Witness tracks awaiting-gate molecules
- [ ] Gate closure triggers molecule resume
- [ ] New polecat picks up from correct step","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-02T13:27:42.298423-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-01-02T13:27:42.298423-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-quw1","depends_on_id":"bd-d1n1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-02T13:28:18.599801-08:00","created_by":"mayor"},{"issue_id":"bd-quw1","depends_on_id":"bd-4k3c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-02T13:28:18.665709-08:00","created_by":"mayor"}]}

-{"id":"bd-z6kw","title":"Refinery gh:run gate auto-discovery","description":"Refinery auto-populates await_id for gh:run gates from recent workflow runs.

## Problem
Gate created by cook has await_type=gh:run but no await_id.
Need to discover the run ID from the push that triggered CI.

## Solution
During Refinery patrol:
1. Find open gh:run gates without await_id
2. Query: gh run list --workflow=<gate.workflow> --limit=5
3. Match run to gate (by branch, commit, or time proximity)
4. Update gate: bd update <gate-id> --await-id=<run-id>
5. Now bd gate check --type=gh can poll that specific run

## Implementation
- Add discover-await-ids step to mol-refinery-patrol
- Use gh CLI to query recent runs
- Match heuristics: same branch, recent commit, within 5 mins
- Update gate with discovered run ID

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Refinery discovers run IDs for gh:run gates
- [ ] Gates updated with correct await_id
- [ ] Subsequent polls use run ID
- [ ] Handles multiple pending gates","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-02T13:27:42.35667-08:00","created_by":"mayor","updated_at":"2026-01-02T13:27:42.35667-08:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-z6kw","depends_on_id":"bd-d1n1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-02T13:28:18.621422-08:00","created_by":"mayor"},{"issue_id":"bd-z6kw","depends_on_id":"bd-4k3c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-02T13:28:18.688305-08:00","created_by":"mayor"}]}

 {"id":"bd-z86n","title":"Code Review: PR #551 - Persist close_reason to issues table","description":"Code review of PR #551 which fixes close_reason persistence bug.

## Summary
The PR correctly fixes a bug where close_reason was only stored in the events table, not in the issues.close_reason column. This caused `bd show --json` to return empty close_reason.

## What Was Fixed
- ✅ CloseIssue now updates both close_reason and closed_at
- ✅ ReOpenIssue clears both close_reason and closed_at
- ✅ Comprehensive tests added for both storage and CLI layers
